headline: Sarawak Election Polls Show GPS Facing Strongest Challenge
context: With Sarawak elections due by 2027 opposition parties mount strongest challenge since 2016 with 35% support in recent polls.

cards.big: Opposition reaches 35% support in Sarawak polls highest since 2016
cards.sub: GPS still leads but urban seats in Kuching and Miri show competitive margins

cards.big: GPS won 76 of 82 seats in 2021 but 18 had margins under 2,000 votes
cards.sub: Younger urban voters trending away from GPS with only 42% under-35 support

cards.big: PSB under Wong Soon Koh targets 25 seats across Chinese-majority constituencies
cards.sub: Party secured 3 seats in 2021 and expanded to 15 branches since then

cards.big: DAP returned to Sarawak operations in 2025 after 3-year hiatus
cards.sub: Federal coalition partner presence complicates GPS unity government relationship

cards.big: Electoral competition tests whether GPS dominance can withstand demographic change
GPS facing its strongest electoral challenge signals that autonomy rhetoric must deliver material results.
